target.name: Dataminr
target.founder: Ted Bailey, Sam Hendel, Jeff Kinsey

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $4.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Applied Intuition
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $15B

data.narrative:
Dataminr is a Series F AI & Software company seeking valuation guidance. The analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 1 public company (Palantir, PLTR) in the same sector and stage cohort, including Applied Intuition, xAI, Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, and Ayar Labs.

Two valuation methods triangulate the fair value range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier to the company's last post-money valuation of $4.1B, yielding $5.3B. Peer-median methodology examines the 8-company comparable set, which shows a median valuation of $4.7B against a range of $1.1B to $250.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within frontier AI—xAI at $250.0B and Applied Intuition at $15.0B anchor the high end, while Ayar Labs at $3.8B sits closer to Dataminr's current $4.1B valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $4.0B to $6.1B, with a median point of $5.0B. This implies modest upside from the current $4.1B post-money, consistent with stage-typical expansion. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity of peer multiples, the limited relevance of the $250B outlier (xAI), and whether Dataminr's business profile—enterprise AI for threat intelligence—commands a premium or discount relative to the broader frontier cohort.
